subject: Public Health
title: Pandemic Preparedness Gaps
description: A 60-second lesson on Pandemic Preparedness Gaps.

hook.text: COVID-19 exposed what experts had warned for decades—the world was not prepared for a pandemic.

buildup.text: Surveillance systems, stockpiles, and response plans existed on paper but crumbled under real-world pressure.

discovery.text: The Global Health Security Index rated no country as 'fully prepared'—even before COVID hit.

twist.text: Countries with the highest ratings—like the US and UK—performed worse than expected during COVID-19.

climax.text: True preparedness requires investment in health workers, labs, supply chains, and trust—not just plans.

punchline.text: A plan without funding is a wish. Preparedness costs money upfront.

quiz.question: What did the Global Health Security Index find before COVID-19?
options: No country was rated as fully prepared
